title: Should You Buy Bloom Energy Stock While It's Below $300?
text: Bloom Energy stock trades at $277.47, approximately 9% below its 52-week high of $302. U.S. data centers consumed approximately 176 terawatt-hours in 2023, with projections reaching 325-580 TWh by 2028. Bloom's competitive advantage lies in deployment speed, with the company able to deploy one of its servers in a company's backyard in as little as 90 days, compared to 4-8 years for grid transmission infrastructure. Q1 2026 revenue grew 130% to $751 million, with full-year guidance between $3.4-$3.8 billion. Valuation remains a significant concern with the stock trading at 128 times forward earnings and a price-to-book ratio of 80, trading like a software company rather than one that builds power equipment. This premium valuation leaves minimal margin for error or market disruptions. The author suggests Bloom warrants consideration for aggressive investors via a small position, while conservative investors might prefer exposure through clean energy ETFs to reduce risk exposure.
